summary: Frontend changes implement /d/{token} link building (with URL encoding) and update copy-link behavior in file tables; PublicDownloadPage now decodes the token and auto-triggers downloads. Backend diff is truncated, so there is no clear evidence that permanent random token generation, uniqueness checks, or numeric-token disabling were implemented.

missing_requirements.requirement: Update the backend to assign each uploaded file a permanent, short, random public download token (not derived from the numeric file ID), and use this token for all public downloads.
missing_requirements.reason: The backend diff excerpt shows the FileMetadata.publicToken field and a tokens map, but the provided truncated content does not include any upload/finalize logic that generates a short random token, ensures it is not derived from the file ID, or checks token uniqueness with collision regeneration.

missing_requirements.requirement: Disable legacy predictable numeric download URLs so that old tokens like "1" no longer work for public downloads, even if they exist in state.
missing_requirements.reason: The truncated backend diff does not show the downloadFile implementation or any explicit rejection of numeric-pattern tokens (e.g., "1"), nor changes demonstrating that numeric tokens are invalid/disabled.

hallucinated_features.description: Public download page automatically triggers a download on page load (in addition to manual button).
hallucinated_features.reason: REQ-3 only specifies routing to the existing public download page and attempting download using the token from the URL; it does not request auto-download behavior or the associated UI state changes.

hallucinated_features.description: Token normalization/encoding behavior added (encode in link builder; decode+trim in PublicDownloadPage).
hallucinated_features.reason: REQ-3 requires link format /d/{token} and using backend-provided token; it does not explicitly request URL encoding/decoding/whitespace trimming logic.
